products.name: AR731 – 38 BHP
products.descriptor: 38 BHP single-rotor Wankel engine for target drones and short-life UAVs

evidence.snippet:
**AR731 – 38 BHP**
---------------------
UAV TARGET ENGINE
The AR731 has the highest power-to-weight ratio of any rotary engine in the world. It has been specifically designed and developed to be the ultimate engine for small target drones and short-life UAV’s. TBO is 10 to 50 hours depending on duty cycle.

Single rotor Wankel-type engine.
CAPACITY
208cc chamber size.
POWER OUTPUT
38bhp at 7,800rpm

products.name: AR741 – 38 BHP
products.descriptor: 38 BHP single-rotor Wankel engine for surveillance UAVs, FAR-33 endurance tested

evidence.snippet:
**AR741 – 38 BHP**
------------------
ENGINE FOR SURVEILLANCE UAVS
The AR741 has an outstanding power-to-weight ratio combined with much longer life and better SFC than any alternative engine. It has been developed from the mature AR731 target drone engine to meet the needs of small surveillance-type UAVs. The engine has successfully completed a 150 hour FAR-33 type endurance test.

Single rotor Wankel-type spark ignition engine.
CAPACITY
208 CC chamber size.
POWER OUTPUT
38bhp (maximum) at 7800 rpm.

products.name: AR682 – 75 BHP
products.descriptor: 75 BHP single-rotor Wankel engine for UAVs with direct-drive propeller constraints

evidence.snippet:
Single rotor Wankel-type spark ignition engine.
CAPACITY
208 CC chamber size.
POWER OUTPUT
38bhp (maximum) at 7800 rpm.

products.name: AR682R – 95 BHP
products.descriptor: 95 BHP twin-rotor Wankel engine with belt reduction drive for high-power UAVs

evidence.snippet:
**AR682r – 95 BHP**
-------------------
ROTARY ENGINE FOR UAVS
The AR682R aero-engine has been developed to power UAVs which require up to 120 bhp. It is based on the proven AR642 core engine which was to meet UK CAA/European JAR-E and US FAA FAR-33 certification standards for manned flight.

Twin rotor Wankel-type rotary engine.
CAPACITY
294 cc per rotor.
POWER OUTPUT
90 bhp at 7000 engine rpm. Alternatively up to 120 bhp at 8000 rpm with EFi System.

products.name: AR801 – 50 BHP
products.descriptor: 50 BHP single-rotor liquid-cooled Wankel engine for drones and UAVs, direct drive

evidence.snippet:
**AR801 – 50 BHP**
------------------
ROTARY ENGINE FOR DRONES & UAVS

Wankel-type rotary, single rotor engine.
CAPACITY
Single rotor – 294cc.
POWER OUTPUT
40 bhp at 6,000 engine rpm. Alternatively 51 bhp at 8,000 rpm (carburettor); or 60 bhp at 8,000 rpm (EFi).

products.name: AR801R – 51 BHP
products.descriptor: 51 BHP single-rotor liquid-cooled Wankel engine with integrated belt reduction drive for UAVs

evidence.snippet:
**AR801R – 51 BHP**
-------------------
ROTARY ENGINE FOR UAVS
The AR801R is a tested and proven, light-weight, single rotor, liquid cooled engine with integrated reduction drive. It has been designed and developed specifically for existing and future UAVs requiring 35 to 55 bhp.

Single rotor Wankel-type engine.
CAPACITY
Single rotor – 294cc.
POWER OUTPUT
40 bhp at 6,000 engine rpm. Alternatively 51 bhp at 8,000 rpm (carburettor); or 60 bhp at 8,000 rpm (EFi).

unresolved_questions: No NDAA or Blue UAS compliance claims found anywhere in the corpus — all products marked unknown for both fields. The quality page references AS9100D certification (aerospace quality standard) but makes no NDAA/Section 889 claims.

The AR682 product page spec section lists 38 BHP at 7800 rpm (matching AR731/AR741 specs) rather than the 75 BHP headline — this appears to be a copy/paste error on the vendor's site; the product name and intro text clearly state 75 BHP at 6000 rpm.

The AR682R product page headline says '95 BHP' but the spec section lists 90 bhp at 7000 rpm (up to 120 bhp with EFi) — the 95 BHP figure in the product name appears to be a nominal/typical rating not exactly reproduced in the spec table.
